methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Bedrock Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Boris Sofman, is valued at $1.75B post-money following $270M in total capital raised. The valuation is assessed against a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company in the robotics sector. Primary peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B to the next financing round, yielding an implied value of $3.3B based on the $1.8B last post-money valuation. Peer-median methodology examines five same-sector, same-stage comparables, which show a median valuation of $500M with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity in the robotics sector at this stage.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $425M (low) to $3.8B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.75B valuation sits near this median, slightly below. Key uncertainties center on the applicability of the peer-median method given the $10.0B outlier (1X Technologies) and sector-wide stage variance, as well as the assumption embedded in stage-progression regarding typical multiplier progression in frontier robotics.
